Title: The Litsea genome and the evolution of the laurel family

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with evolution of inflorescence architecture in Lauraceae rather than nutrient metabolite accumulation
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NHL domain-containing protein; two or three conserved NHL domains reported
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FUWA homolog phylogeny matched divergence of inflorescence types in Lauraceae. 2. Early-diverging lineages carried FUWA proteins with two NHL domains, whereas other Lauraceae species had three NHL domains. 3. This gene was discussed for floral architecture evolution, not nutrient metabolism.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it is a core experimentally discussed gene in Results, but it is not a nutrient metabolism gene.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression pattern associated with perianth tube morphology, not nutrient metabolism
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in flower buds of Lauraceae clades with turbinate or suburceolate perianth tubes; lower in Litsea-Cinnamomum clade
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PTL expression differed among Lauraceae clades. 2. Higher PTL expression correlated with more developed perianth tubes. 3. The gene was linked to floral morphology rather than nutrient metabolite regulation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a nutrient metabolism gene; retained only because it is a core Result gene.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with male flower development; no direct nutrient metabolite effect shown
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in male flowers than in female flowers of monoecious species and bisexual flowers of dioecious species
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TGA10 was among DEGs enriched in plant hormone signal transduction. 2. It showed consistently higher expression in male flowers across Lauraceae species. 3. The paper suggests involvement in sexual determination, not nutrient metabolism.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a nutrient metabolism gene; included as a core Result gene outside metabolism.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Hypothesized contribution to sexual determination; no nutrient metabolism role established
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Differentially expressed between bisexual and unisexual flowers
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A hypothesized protein Lcu01G_02292 was identified among flower-bud DEGs. 2. Its expression pattern suggested a possible role in sexual determination. 3. No causal role in nutrient metabolism was shown.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Plastidial isoprenoid precursor supply for monoterpene biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses pyruvate and glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate to form 1-deoxy-D-xylulose 5-phosphate, supplying the MEP pathway
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Scents used in perfumes, cosmetics, and medicine; potential defense-related volatiles
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as first key enzyme in scent biosynthetic pathway
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: DXS; thiamine pyrophosphate-dependent enzyme; clade B member
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome expression profiling; transient overexpression in L. cubeba; volatile metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DXS clade B members are expanded in Lauraceae lineages including Litsea. 2. LcuDXS3 is highly expressed in fruits. 3. Transient overexpression of LcuDXS3 increased several monoterpene and sesquiterpene components, supporting precursor-supply control of terpenoid accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of several monoterpene and sesquiterpene volatiles
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful upstream engineering target to enhance essential oil yield, especially when combined with downstream TPS genes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper mentions LcuDXS3-5 as clade B members; only LcuDXS3 was functionally overexpressed.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Litsea cubeba; Nicotiana benthamiana; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Litsea cubeba; Nicotiana benthamiana; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes or rearranges geranyl diphosphate to generate multiple leaf volatile monoterpenes
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Volatile scent compounds contributing to Lauraceae leaf aroma and likely defense
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: TPSs described in paper as rate-limiting enzymes for terpenoid production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; transient overexpression; recombinant enzyme assay with GPP; GC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ABA treatment of L. cubeba leaves increased LcuTPS22 expression and monoterpene levels
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LcuTPS22 is a leaf-expressed TPS-b gene in expanded Lauraceae TPS family. 2. Transient expression and in vitro enzyme assay showed that it produces alpha-pinene, beta-pinene, eucalyptol, camphene, and camphor. 3. ABA treatment increased both LcuTPS22 expression and monoterpene accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of major leaf volatile monoterpenes
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering leaf essential oil composition or increasing aroma/defense volatiles; may be combined with ABA-responsive regulation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Applied species for functional assay included tobacco leaves and recombinant E. coli expression, while source gene is from L. cubeba.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Litsea cubeba; Nicotiana benthamiana; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Litsea cubeba; Nicotiana benthamiana; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts geranyl diphosphate into linalool, phellandrene, and geraniol
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major components of specific scents in Lauraceae; used commercially in fragrances, cosmetics, and medicine
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: TPSs described as rate-limiting enzymes for terpenoid production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression; recombinant enzyme assay with GPP; GC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LcuTPS42 is highly expressed in L. cubeba fruits. 2. Transient expression and enzyme assays demonstrated biosynthesis of linalool, phellandrene, and geraniol. 3. These products are major scent compounds in Lauraceae.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of key fruit scent monoterpenes
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for fruit essential oil quality engineering and for increasing desirable fragrance compounds
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Function was directly verified both in vivo and in vitro.
